name: Tete Montoliu
abstract: Vicenç Montoliu i Massana, better known as Tete Montoliu (28 March 1933 – 24 August 1997) was a Spanish jazz pianist from Catalonia, Spain. Born blind, he learnt braille music at age seven.

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: We did a lot of traveling over in Europe and the United States, but a lot of traveling especially in Spain... Tete – we had this tour set up. So I fly over to – I fly to Spain.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Except for the first time at the Jamboree, the pianist was a blind Spanish fellow named Tete Montoliu... That band really wailed.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Forget about Tete Montoliu or Jon Christensen. Forget about Spain and Sweden.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
